name: Subida Castillo de Cote — circular by the Sierra de San Pablo
name_original: Subida Castillo de Cote — circular por la Sierra de San Pablo
region_intro: Hiking around Montellano offers diverse landscapes, from varied elevations to significant natural features. The region is characterized by rolling hills, limestone cliffs, and deep gorges carved by rivers. Trails often traverse old railway lines, providing unique perspectives on the area's geology and history. This part of Andalusia presents a range of paths suitable for different skill levels, from moderate climbs to longer treks. Best hiking trails around Montellano The most popular hiking route is Sierra de San Pablo y Montellano — circular from the population of Montellano, a 6.6 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete, offering impressive views over the surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Subida Castillo de Cote — circular by the Sierra de San Pablo, a moderate 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path leading to the historic Castillo de Cote with panoramic vistas. Local hikers also love the Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail, an 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail following an old railway line through tunnels and viaducts, often completed in about 4 hours 48 minutes. Hiking around Montellano is defined by varied terrain, limestone cliffs, and old railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Montellano are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Montellano's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Montellano?
A: There are over a dozen hiking trails around Montellano, offering diverse experiences for various skill levels. The region features 17 routes, with the majority being moderate, ensuring a good balance of accessibility and challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Montellano's hiking trails?
A: Montellano's hiking trails feature varied terrain, including rolling hills, limestone cliffs, and deep gorges carved by rivers. You'll find paths traversing old railway lines, offering unique perspectives on the area's geology and history, alongside more traditional mountain paths.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Montellano?
A: While most routes are moderate, the region does offer paths suitable for beginners. For a generally accessible experience, consider sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009104432/via-verde-de-la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which follows an old railway line and has a gentler gradient.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking in Montellano?
A: Montellano is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the dramatic cliffs and the Guadalporcún River's gorge within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, known for its Griffon Vulture colony. The ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3358115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chaparro de la Vega&lt;/a&gt; oak tree is another significant natural monument. Additionally, the historic Castillo de Coto offers panoramic vistas, accessible via routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009133391/subida-castillo-de-cote-circular-by-the-sierra-de-san-pablo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subida Castillo de Cote — circular by the Sierra de San Pablo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Montellano?
A: Yes, Montellano offers several excellent circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009130864/sierra-de-san-pablo-y-montellano-circular-from-the-population-of-montellano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pablo y Montellano — circular from the population of Montellano&lt;/a&gt;, which provides impressive views over the surrounding hills. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009133391/subida-castillo-de-cote-circular-by-the-sierra-de-san-pablo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subida Castillo de Cote — circular by the Sierra de San Pablo&lt;/a&gt;, leading to the historic Castillo de Cote.

Q: Can I see any wildlife, especially birds, on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is particularly renowned for hosting the largest colony of Griffon Vultures in Andalusia and one of the largest in Europe. You'll often spot these majestic birds soaring above the cliffs, especially along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009104432/via-verde-de-la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009104432/via-verde-de-la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent example. This 36-kilometer route follows an old railway line, featuring tunnels and viaducts, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and historical significance as it passes through peaceful landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Montellano?
A: The trails in Montellano are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the impressive views, and the unique experience of routes like the Vía Verde de la Sierra.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or unique geological formations?
A: Yes, within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Zaframagón Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, the 'Garganta del Estrechón' gorge, carved by the Guadalporcún River, is known for its unique geological formations. Here, you can discover natural pools, 'marmitas de gigante' (giant's kettles), and small waterfalls, especially after rainfall.

Q: Are there any routes that connect to other towns or villages?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009104432/via-verde-de-la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent option for this, as it traverses the Sierra de Cádiz and passes through smaller villages like Coripe. You can also find specific loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19321662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra – Coripe loop from Coripe&lt;/a&gt;, which starts and ends in Coripe.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montellano?
A: Montellano offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during those months. Winters are mild, making it possible to hike year-round.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Many trails in Montellano offer spectacular views due to the varied elevations. The circular route around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009130864/sierra-de-san-pablo-y-montellano-circular-from-the-population-of-montellano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pablo y Montellano&lt;/a&gt; provides impressive vistas. Additionally, the historic Castillo de Coto, reached via routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009133391/subida-castillo-de-cote-circular-by-the-sierra-de-san-pablo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subida Castillo de Cote&lt;/a&gt;, offers panoramic views over the surrounding landscape.
tips: technical and hard path to get to the castle.
This viewpoint is part of a hiking route that also includes the Mirador de la Cancha and the Castillo de Cote. The complete route is approximately 11.5 km and is circular.
